ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry exists for mango-mandarin blended beverage; closest_parent 'fruit juice (food product)' used as ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Fruit nectars and drinks) is the closest defensible parent category for a mango-mandarin blended fruit drink. No exact code exists for this specific blend.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 'Fruit drink' is the closest parent concept; no exact code for mango-mandarin blend exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 250 ml (one standard cup or bottle), as typically served at Kazo Premium outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mango juice, canned or bottled, unsweetened — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168185); Mandarin orange juice — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 169105); values blended proportionally (50% mango juice, 50% mandarin juice) to represent a mango-mandarin fruit drink.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Kazo Premium Mango Mandarin Kazu Kazu exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy data for mango juice (FDC 168185) and mandarin orange juice (FDC 169105) at an assumed 50:50 ratio. This is a reasonable approximation for a mango-mandarin fruit drink. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Mango juice and mandarin/orange juice GI values — University of Sydney GI Database; mango juice GI ~47 (Atkinson et al., 2008); orange/mandarin juice GI ~50; blended estimate ~47–50, assigned 47 (low category).
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kazo Premium Mango Mandarin Kazu Kazu. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for mango juice (~47, low) and orange/mandarin juice (~50, low). Blended estimate assigned as 47 (low GI). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (47 × 12.2 × 2.5) / 100 = 14.4 g per 250 ml serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~12.5 mg per 250 ml serving); naturally rich in potassium (~325 mg per serving) which supports blood pressure regulation.
hypertension.guidance: The low sodium and moderate potassium content of this mango-mandarin drink make it generally suitable for individuals with hypertension, though the natural sugar content should be considered in the context of overall dietary intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~14.4 per 250 ml serving) driven by natural fruit sugars (~27 g sugar per serving); low GI (47) but portion size amplifies glycemic impact.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this mango-mandarin fruit drink in moderation, as the natural fruit sugars contribute a moderate glycemic load per serving; limiting to half a serving or pairing with a protein or fibre source can help blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; provides vitamin C (~45 mg per serving) and potassium (~325 mg per serving), both associated with cardiovascular benefit.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This mango-mandarin drink is low in saturated fat and sodium, and provides antioxidant vitamin C and potassium, making it a heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed as part of a balanced diet and without excessive added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mango Mandarin Kazu Kazu is a chilled fruit beverage sold by Kazo Premium, a Singapore-based premium fruit drink brand with an outlet at Funan Mall. The drink is a blend of ripe mango and mandarin orange juice, delivering a sweet, tangy, and refreshing tropical flavour profile. It is typically served cold in a single-serve cup or bottle of approximately 250 ml. The beverage is minimally processed, made from real fruit without significant dairy or grain components, and is suitable for vegetarians and vegans. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 125 kcal per serving, with the dominant macronutrient being natural fruit sugars (~27 g per serving). It is low in fat, low in sodium, and provides meaningful amounts of vitamin C (~45 mg), potassium (~325 mg), and vitamin A (~37.5 mcg RAE) per serving. The glycemic index is estimated at 47 (low), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~14.4) due to the volume of natural sugars. Clinically, this drink is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate their intake due to the natural sugar content. The drink is a popular grab-and-go option in Singapore's food court and mall culture, reflecting the city-state's vibrant fresh fruit beverage scene.

How many calories in Kazo Premium Mango Mandarin drink?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Kazo Premium Mango Mandarin Kazu Kazu exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary approved nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy data for mango juice and mandarin orange juice at a 50:50 ratio. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for mango juice and orange juice. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for mango juice (FDC 168185) and mandarin orange juice (FDC 169105) blended at an assumed 50:50 ratio, as no exact product entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source data for fruit juices; naturally zero for this fruit juice blend). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero; fruit-based beverages contain no vitamin B12). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero; fruit juices do not contain vitamin D unless fortified; no fortification indicated for this product). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero; plant-based fruit beverage). monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are 0.0 (confirmed trace/negligible per USDA SR Legacy for fruit juices). trans_fat_g is 0.0 (naturally zero; no hydrogenated fats in fruit juice). GI of 47 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (mango juice ~47, orange juice ~50); assigned 47 as conservative low-GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving = (47 × 12.2 × 2.5) / 100 = 14.4. Serving size assumed 250 ml based on standard single-serve fruit drink portion in Singapore. Price SGD 5.80 per piece as per GrabFood listing. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact ontology entry exists for this specific mango-mandarin blend product.
